title: Alsophila percrassa
taxonomy: Alsophila percrassa is classified within the family Cyatheaaceae, commonly referred to as the tree fern family. It belongs to the genus Alsophila, which is a group of ferns often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Alsophila percrassa is a species of fern that is native to New Zealand. The plant is a type of tree fern, characterized by its tall, woody stems and large, lobed leaves. Alsophila percrassa is often found growing in shaded, humid areas with rich soil.

intro: Alsophila percrassa, also known as the New Caledonian tree fern, is a species of plant in the family Cyatheaceae, characterized by its tall, woody stems and large, lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Alsophila percrassa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds, which are thought to be responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
